brand.name: BuildMyWebNow
brand.voice: Direct, practical, veteran-owned small business talking to other small business owners. No corporate fluff, no jargon. Confident but never salesy. Short sentences.

pillars.goal: Teach one actionable local SEO tactic a High Desert business owner can use this week.
topics: Google Business Profile: why responding to every review boosts local ranking
The one line every website needs: city + service in the page title
Why 'near me' searches skip businesses without a website
How photos on your Google listing affect calls
Why your hours being wrong online costs you customers
What a sitemap is and why Google cares
Why Facebook-only businesses are invisible on Google
How to pick the right business category on Google Maps

pillars.goal: Share one true, verifiable statistic about small business websites or local search, framed for High Desert owners. Only use statistics you are confident are real and current; if unsure, use a conservative widely-cited figure.
topics: Percentage of consumers who search online before visiting a local business
How many people judge a business's credibility by its website
Mobile share of local searches
How fast visitors leave a slow-loading site
Share of small businesses that still have no website
How often a Google Business Profile with a website link gets more clicks

pillars.goal: Bust one common myth small business owners believe about websites.
topics: Myth: 'My customers are all word-of-mouth, I don't need a website'
Myth: 'A Facebook page is the same as a website'
Myth: 'Websites cost thousands and take months'
Myth: 'Nobody in a small town searches online'
Myth: 'I need to post content constantly for a website to work'
Myth: 'Yelp has me covered'

pillars.goal: Show that BuildMyWebNow builds websites for a specific business category. CRITICAL: the headline must be from BuildMyWebNow's perspective selling web design to that business owner, NOT ad copy for the business itself. The headline says what a website DOES for that kind of business (get found, get calls, get bookings) and implicitly that we build it. Good headline: 'HVAC sites that turn Google searches into calls'. Bad headline: 'Emergency AC repair you can trust' (that is the HVAC company's own ad, not ours). The browser mockup shows a sample site we would build for that category; the headline sells OUR service.
topics: Barbershops: a site that turns followers into booked chairs
HVAC companies: a site that captures the emergency Google search
Salons: a booking page that works while the owner sleeps
Restaurants: a site with a menu that never looks like a blurry photo
Auto shops: a site that pulls in estimate requests
Real estate: a fast site that loads listings on any phone
Dental offices: new patient forms before they arrive

pillars.goal: Give a 10-minute action any business owner can do today to improve their online presence, no website required. Builds trust by being useful with no strings attached.
topics: Claim your free Google Business Profile in 10 minutes
Ask your 3 happiest customers for a Google review today
Add your phone number to your Instagram bio
Take 5 photos of your work and post them to your Google listing
Google your own business name and see what customers see
Make sure your holiday hours are updated online
